treatment_information.treatment_response: Reasonably controlled chronic pelvic pain but dissatisfied due to weight gain and loss of libido

treatment_information.specific_treatment: total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy
treatment_information.treatment_response: Excellent pain relief and complete resolution of disease confirmed by second-look laparoscopy

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with chronic pelvic pain, managed with megestrol
Diagnostic laparoscopy revealed peritoneal studding concerning for malignancy
Frozen section of implants returned nondiagnostic; permanent sections read as benign spindle cell proliferation
Definitive surgical management with total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy performed
Second-look laparoscopy performed nearly 1 year after surgery showed complete resolution of disease

patient_outcome.final_status: Complete resolution of disease with no evidence of bowel implants
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Second-look laparoscopy performed nearly 1 year after definitive therapy
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Definitive treatment with disease regression, though recurrence in small bowel involvement has been documented in a small number of patients
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Dissatisfied with hormonal therapy due to weight gain and loss of libido
